---
title: "Data_Cleaning_Assignment"
output: 
  pdf_document:
      latex_engine: xelatex
date: "2026-04-01"
---

```{r}
## 1. Data set overview
# Reading data sets
netflix_data <- read.csv("Data/netflix_titles.csv")
imdb_data <- read.csv("Data/TMDB_all_movies.csv")

# data overview
head(netflix_data)
str(netflix_data)
summary(netflix_data)

head(imdb_data)
str(imdb_data)
summary(imdb_data)

# Notes
# imdb_rating has 712327 missing values, def need to fix that.
# netflix release year is int
# imdb's release year is chr




## 2. Variable 1 Cleaning: Cleaning Title and Year 
#install.packages("dplyr")
#install.packages("stringr")
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

# cleaning title
netflix_clean <- netflix_data %>% 
  mutate(title_clean = str_to_lower(title) %>%
         str_trim() %>%
         str_replace_all("[^a-z0-9 ]", "") %>%
         str_squish())

imdb_clean <- imdb_data %>% 
  mutate(title_clean = str_to_lower(title) %>%
         str_trim() %>%
         str_replace_all("[^a-z0-9 ]", "") %>%
         str_squish())

# cleaning release year
imdb_clean <- imdb_clean %>%
  mutate(release_year = as.numeric(substr(release_date, 1, 4))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(release_year),
         release_year >= 1900,
         release_year <= 2021)

summary(netflix_clean$release_year)
summary(imdb_clean$release_year)


# Notes
# we will be merging on title and release year so we need to clean those 2 cols before joining
# str_to_lower(title) conveets all letters to lowercase
# str_trim() removes extra spaces at the beginning and end
# str_replace_all("[^a-z0-9 ]", "") removes everything that is not lowercase letters, numbers (0-9) or spaces. so just removes punctuations so that dosent interfere with the join.
# str_squish()) remoes extra spaces between words
# substr(release_date, 1, 4) takes first 4 characters so the year
# as.numeric() will convert chr to int
# after converting imdb's release year to int when i did summary i saw min year was 1874 and max year was 2069 so i limitied the clean release year to 1900-2021



## 3. Variable 2 Cleaning: Cleaning imdb rating and votes
imdb_clean <- imdb_clean %>%
  filter(!is.na(imdb_rating),
         !is.na(imdb_votes),
         imdb_votes > 50)
         
# notes:
# dropping imdb ratings and votes that are null and setting limit to imdb votes

## 4. Variable 3 Cleaning: Merging the two datasets
# We use an inner join to keep only the movies that exist in BOTH datasets
merged_data <- inner_join(netflix_clean, imdb_clean, by = c("title_clean", "release_year"))

# Removing any duplicate entries that may have occurred during the join
merged_data <- merged_data %>% 
  distinct(title_clean, release_year, .keep_all = TRUE)


## 5. Before vs After Comparison
# Installing ggplot2 for visualizations if not already installed
# install.packages("ggplot2")
library(ggplot2)

# --- STATS & TABLES ---
# Calculate rows before and after
rows_before_netflix <- nrow(netflix_data)
rows_before_imdb <- nrow(imdb_data)
rows_after_merge <- nrow(merged_data)

# Missing values in IMDB ratings before and after
missing_ratings_before <- sum(is.na(imdb_data$imdb_rating))
missing_ratings_after <- sum(is.na(merged_data$imdb_rating))

# Create a clean summary table for the report
comparison_table <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("Total Netflix Rows", "Total IMDB Rows", "Merged Rows", "Missing IMDB Ratings"),
  Before_Cleaning = c(rows_before_netflix, rows_before_imdb, "N/A", missing_ratings_before),
  After_Cleaning = c("N/A", "N/A", rows_after_merge, missing_ratings_after)
)

# Print the table using knitr for a clean PDF look
knitr::kable(comparison_table, caption = "Data Summary Before and After Cleaning")

# --- VISUALIZATIONS ---
# Boxplot comparing the distribution of IMDB ratings before vs after cleaning
# We create a temporary dataframe just for the plot
plot_data_before <- data.frame(Rating = imdb_data$imdb_rating, State = "Before Cleaning (Raw IMDB)")
plot_data_after <- data.frame(Rating = merged_data$imdb_rating, State = "After Cleaning & Merging")
plot_data <- rbind(plot_data_before[!is.na(plot_data_before$Rating), ], plot_data_after)

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = State, y = Rating, fill = State)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Distribution of IMDB Ratings Before and After Data Cleaning",
       x = "Dataset State",
       y = "IMDB Rating") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```
